title: Ladenbergia macrocarpa
taxonomy: Ladenbergia macrocarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family comprises over 13,000 species, making it one of the largest flowering plant families. Ladenbergia macrocarpa is a member of this family due to its shared characteristics with other Rubiaceae species. The classification of Ladenbergia macrocarpa within the Rubiaceae family is based on its botanical features and molecular analysis.

intro: Ladenbergia macrocarpa, also known as Indian alkanet, is a plant species native to the Himalayan regions, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues.
